title: Zero roadkill as Amazon canopy bridges secure 15,000 crossings
excerpt: For some of the world's rarest monkeys, the path to survival now runs through a simple network of ropes.
content_html:
• Project Reconecta installed eight rope bridges in a municipality in Brazil, and a 15-month study used camera traps to document thousands of arboreal wildlife adapting to new aerial crossings.
  • Safe road passages enable seed dispersal and gene diversity, reversing the “domino effect” of biodiversity loss that begins when highways split habitats.
  • Reconecta’s creator, biologist Fernanda Abra, a National Geographic Explorer and winner of the Whitley Award, is now expanding these low-cost conservation tools to other Brazilian biomes and neighboring Suriname.
  • Brazil’s federal transport department has officially designated the rope bridge design as the recommended national standard for highways to curb the millions of wildlife deaths recorded on roads annually.

Around 7 a.m., with dense fog blanketing the landscape, biologist Gabriel Falquetto had already come across several road-killed animals along ES-164 highway in Brazil’s Espírito Santo state. He was on his way to Kaetés Private Natural Heritage Reserve (RPPN), in the Atlantic Forest, where he monitors wildlife and roadkill in the region.

Suddenly, he spotted the lifeless body of a primate on the pavement. It was a buffy-headed marmoset (Callithrix flaviceps), a species listed as critically endangered on the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) Red List. An estimated 2,500 individuals remain in the wild, making it one of the world’s most threatened primates.

“It was extremely painful and discouraging,” Falquetto told Mongabay. “We travel through this region every day monitoring wildlife, and we celebrate every encounter with groups of buffy-headed marmosets in the forest.”

“Finding an individual in those conditions brings a feeling of sadness and helplessness. Beyond the loss of that animal, the incident reminds us of the impacts our activities have on biodiversity.”

About 2,900 kilometers (1,800 miles) away, on the road that leads to Alta Floresta, a small Amazon town in the far north of Mato Grosso state, cases like the buffy-headed marmoset’s roadkill have not been recorded since 2024. That year, eight canopy bridges were installed, connecting fragmented forest patches. Camera trap footage revealed that, over 15 months, the aerial wildlife crossings enabled 15,000 safe crossings by arboreal animals — and not a single roadkill.

The bridges are part of a program involving Projeto Reconecta and the municipal government, along with local organizations and business owners who are eager to conserve the unique biodiversity of their town and promote ecotourism, but were alarmed by the growing number of wildlife-vehicle collisions.

Biologist Fernanda Abra, an associate researcher at the Smithsonian National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute in the U.S., and the founder of Reconecta, recalled that just hours before installing the first bridges in Alta Floresta, she found two Schneider’s marmosets (Mico schneideri) dead. The species is endemic to the Brazilian Amazon and is threatened with extinction.

“A canopy bridge serves two purposes,” Abra said. “The first is to improve connectivity for wildlife, allowing animals to move from one side to the other in these fragmented forest areas. As a result, we also reduce roadkill.”

All bridges have two camera traps, one facing the crossing to record which animals are using it and another facing the forest to observe which animals arrive there but decide not to use it.

Among the primate species recorded using the bridges during the first months of the program were the black-faced black spider monkey (Ateles chamek), the Purus red howler monkey (Alouatta puruensis), the northern night monkey (Aotus infulatus), the tufted capuchin (Sapajus apella), Schneider’s marmoset and the Alta Floresta titi monkey (Plecturocebus grovesi).

Described only in 2019 and soon after classified as critically endangered, the Alta Floresta titi monkey has become the symbol of the initiative, which is now entering a new phase thanks to its positive results.

“In August, we will install eight more bridges in the city’s urban area,” Abra said. “The only difference is that the electric utility will first need to lower the power lines and insulate them so that if animals jump from the bridge onto the wires, they won’t be electrocuted.”

Bridge use increases over time

The project underway in Alta Floresta is not Reconecta’s first. In 2021, working alongside the Waimiri-Atroari Indigenous people, the team installed 32 canopy bridges over BR-174, the highway that crosses both their territory and the Amazon between the Brazilian states of Amazonas and Roraima.

The Waimiri-Atroari helped Abra identify the best locations for the artificial bridges. Over the past five years, cameras mounted on the bridge poles have documented about 1,250 primate crossings, including the golden-handed tamarin (Saguinus midas), a species that is not strictly arboreal and often descends to the ground in search of food, making it particularly vulnerable to roadkill.

“The outcomes of the BR-174 project have been incredible. We were able to test different bridge designs to find out which ones the animals prefer,” Abra said.

After years of working along Brazilian highways, the biologist had always sought a solution that was simple, low-cost and durable. The current bridge features a multilayer design that accommodates primates with different modes of locomotion — from species that rely on their prehensile tails to move through the canopy to brachiating species such as spider monkeys, which swing using their arms, both of which are common in the Amazon, as well as species that simply walk across the network of crossed ropes. It is worth noting that other small terrestrial mammals, including opossums, sloths and mouse opossums, have also been recorded using the bridges.

Abra said it is important to keep in mind that this is a long-term project, and results are not always immediate. BR-174 was built in the 1970s, and animals take time to adapt to a new crossing alternative. Reconecta’s monitoring has shown that use of the structures increases over time.

“Very often, our sense of urgency in conservation doesn’t take into account the timing of the impact,” she said.

In Alta Floresta, tufted capuchins took seven months before using one of the bridges for the first time, which may indicate that each species has its own pace of adaptation.

Species disappear, forests decline

Home to the world’s greatest diversity of primates, Brazil also has the world’s fourth-largest road network — a deadly combination for animals living along forest edges that have been split apart to make way for highways.

“From our human perspective, roads mean connection and development. But for wildlife, they represent disconnection, barriers and a threat to survival,” Abra said.

Nearly 9 million mammals are killed on Brazil’s roads every year, a 2022 study found. A data compilation published in the journal Nature in 2025 also found that the country ranks among those with the highest mortality rates of terrestrial vertebrates killed in roadkills.

“Habitat loss is one of the main drivers of population declines, especially among threatened species, but the impact of roadkill is still greatly underestimated,” primatologist Fabiano Rodrigues de Melo, a professor at the Federal University of Viçosa in Minas Gerais, told Mongabay.

Beyond roadkills, highways bring confinement to fauna.

“When populations become isolated, they grow smaller and more vulnerable to threats,” said Amely Branquinho Martins, an environmental analyst at the ICMBio, Brazil’s federal agency for conservation units, and coordinator of the Brazilian Biodiversity Genomics (GBB) project.

One of those threats is inbreeding, which occurs when closely related individuals reproduce. “Habitat loss and degradation can prevent an individual from one population from migrating to another — what we call gene flow. Without it, the species’ genetic diversity declines.”

The consequences continue to multiply in a domino effect, Melo said. The loss of primates, especially fruit-eating species, can alter forest composition, because they play a key role in seed dispersal and even contribute to pollination.

“A whole network of ecological interactions is ultimately lost with the disappearance of these species,” Melo said.

Reconecta expands

One of the hopes for reducing wildlife roadkill is the passage of a bill currently under consideration in Brazil’s Congress that would establish the National Road Safety Plan for Wildlife, which calls, among other measures, for the placement of wildlife warning signs, speed-reduction devices and the construction of overpasses, aerial crossings and underpasses along highways.

The bill has already been approved by the Chamber of Deputies and is now awaiting a vote in the Senate.

If approved, the canopy bridge model developed by Reconecta could be implemented nationwide. In 2026, Brazil’s National Department of Transport Infrastructure designated the design as the recommended standard for highways.

In addition to Reconecta expansion results, Abra’s efforts to protect primates have earned international recognition. In 2019, she received the Future For Nature Award, followed by the Whitley Award in 2024, often referred to as the Oscars of environmental conservation. In June, she was also named one of the recipients of the 2026 Wayfinder Award, joining the select group of National Geographic Explorers, who receive funding from the organization for their projects.

The support comes at the right time, as Abra plans to expand Reconecta, including new chapters in Suriname and Lucas do Rio Verde, a municipality in Brazil, which will install 10 canopy bridges.

Abra also hopes to bring her canopy bridges to other biomes, including the Pantanal and the Atlantic Forest, helping prevent the deaths of monkeys such as the buffy-headed marmoset.

Expectations are especially high for bridges over BR-262, one of Brazil’s longest highways, which cuts across the Pantanal and is known as the “highway of death.” The road kills approximately 2,000 animals every year.

“I envision a future with Reconecta Biomes. We already have Reconecta Amazon, and we’ll soon launch Reconecta Pantanal. I also see canopy bridges in the Atlantic Forest for the primates that live there, including muriquis and howler monkeys, which are both highly threatened,” she said.

She stressed that for this vision to succeed, the continued engagement of government agencies, private companies and local communities will be essential, just as it was in Alta Floresta and the Waimiri-Atroari Indigenous Territory. “When everyone is involved, everyone values the initiative and works harder to make it succeed.”

Banner image: A black-faced black spider monkey (Ateles chamek) crossing one of the multilayered bridges. Image courtesy of Sergio Leal. 

How effective are canopy bridges really?

blocks.html_en: • Project Reconecta installed eight rope bridges in a municipality in Brazil, and a 15-month study used camera traps to document thousands of arboreal wildlife adapting to new aerial crossings.

sentences.en: Project Reconecta installed eight rope bridges in a municipality in Brazil, and a 15-month study used camera traps to document thousands of arboreal wildlife adapting to new aerial crossings.
sentences.zh: Reconecta 项目在巴西的一个城市安装了八座索桥，一项为期 15 个月的研究使用相机陷阱记录了数千种树栖野生动物如何适应新的空中穿越。

blocks.html_en: • Safe road passages enable seed dispersal and gene diversity, reversing the “domino effect” of biodiversity loss that begins when highways split habitats.

sentences.en: Safe road passages enable seed dispersal and gene diversity, reversing the “domino effect” of biodiversity loss that begins when highways split habitats.
sentences.zh: 安全的道路通道可以促进种子传播和基因多样性，扭转因高速公路分裂栖息地而产生的生物多样性丧失的“多米诺骨牌效应”。

blocks.html_en: • Reconecta’s creator, biologist Fernanda Abra, a National Geographic Explorer and winner of the Whitley Award, is now expanding these low-cost conservation tools to other Brazilian biomes and neighboring Suriname.

sentences.en: Reconecta’s creator, biologist Fernanda Abra, a National Geographic Explorer and winner of the Whitley Award, is now expanding these low-cost conservation tools to other Brazilian biomes and neighboring Suriname.
sentences.zh: Reconecta 的创建者、生物学家费尔南达·阿布拉 (Fernanda Abra)、国家地理探险家和惠特利奖获得者，目前正在将这些低成本保护工具扩展到其他巴西生物群落和邻国苏里南。

blocks.html_en: • Brazil’s federal transport department has officially designated the rope bridge design as the recommended national standard for highways to curb the millions of wildlife deaths recorded on roads annually.

sentences.en: Brazil’s federal transport department has officially designated the rope bridge design as the recommended national standard for highways to curb the millions of wildlife deaths recorded on roads annually.
sentences.zh: 巴西联邦交通部门已正式将索桥设计指定为高速公路的推荐国家标准，以遏制每年数以百万计的野生动物在道路上死亡的情况。

The species is endemic to the Brazilian Amazon and is threatened with extinction.

sentences.en: Biologist Fernanda Abra, an associate researcher at the Smithsonian National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute in the U.S., and the founder of Reconecta, recalled that just hours before installing the first bridges in Alta Floresta, she found two Schneider’s marmosets (Mico schneideri) dead.
sentences.zh: 美国史密森尼国家动物园和保护生物学研究所副研究员、Reconecta 创始人、生物学家费尔南达·阿布拉 (Fernanda Abra) 回忆道，就在上弗洛雷斯塔安装第一座桥梁的前几个小时，她发现两只施耐德狨猴 (Mico schneideri) 死亡。

sentences.en: The species is endemic to the Brazilian Amazon and is threatened with extinction.
sentences.zh: 该物种是巴西亚马逊地区的特有物种，面临灭绝的威胁。

blocks.html_en: “A canopy bridge serves two purposes,” Abra said. “The first is to improve connectivity for wildlife, allowing animals to move from one side to the other in these fragmented forest areas. As a result, we also reduce roadkill.”

sentences.en: “A canopy bridge serves two purposes,” Abra said.
sentences.zh: “天篷桥有两个目的，”阿布拉说。

sentences.en: “The first is to improve connectivity for wildlife, allowing animals to move from one side to the other in these fragmented forest areas.
sentences.zh: “首先是改善野生动物的连通性，允许动物在这些支离破碎的森林地区从一侧移动到另一侧。

sentences.en: As a result, we also reduce roadkill.”
sentences.zh: 因此，我们也减少了道路交通事故。”

blocks.html_en: All bridges have two camera traps, one facing the crossing to record which animals are using it and another facing the forest to observe which animals arrive there but decide not to use it.

sentences.en: All bridges have two camera traps, one facing the crossing to record which animals are using it and another facing the forest to observe which animals arrive there but decide not to use it.
sentences.zh: 所有桥梁都有两个相机陷阱，一个面向十字路口以记录哪些动物正在使用它，另一个面向森林以观察哪些动物到达那里但决定不使用它。

blocks.html_en: Among the primate species recorded using the bridges during the first months of the program were the black-faced black spider monkey (Ateles chamek), the Purus red howler monkey (Alouatta puruensis), the northern night monkey (Aotus infulatus), the tufted capuchin (Sapajus apella), Schneider’s marmoset and the Alta Floresta titi monkey (Plecturocebus grovesi).

sentences.en: Among the primate species recorded using the bridges during the first months of the program were the black-faced black spider monkey (Ateles chamek), the Purus red howler monkey (Alouatta puruensis), the northern night monkey (Aotus infulatus), the tufted capuchin (Sapajus apella), Schneider’s marmoset and the Alta Floresta titi monkey (Plecturocebus grovesi).
sentences.zh: 在该计划的头几个月里，使用桥梁记录到的灵长类动物包括黑面黑蜘蛛猴 (Ateles chamek)、红吼猴 (Alouatta puruensis)、北夜猴 (Aotus infulatus)、簇绒卷尾猴 (Sapajus apella)、施耐德狨猴和阿尔塔花猴 (Plecturocebus grovesi)。

blocks.html_en: Described only in 2019 and soon after classified as critically endangered, the Alta Floresta titi monkey has become the symbol of the initiative, which is now entering a new phase thanks to its positive results.

sentences.en: Described only in 2019 and soon after classified as critically endangered, the Alta Floresta titi monkey has become the symbol of the initiative, which is now entering a new phase thanks to its positive results.
sentences.zh: Alta Floresta 蒂蒂猴在 2019 年才被描述为极度濒危物种，不久后就已成为该倡议的象征，由于其积极成果，该倡议目前正在进入一个新阶段。

blocks.html_en: “In August, we will install eight more bridges in the city’s urban area,” Abra said. “The only difference is that the electric utility will first need to lower the power lines and insulate them so that if animals jump from the bridge onto the wires, they won’t be electrocuted.”

sentences.en: “In August, we will install eight more bridges in the city’s urban area,” Abra said.
sentences.zh: “八月份，我们将在该市市区再安装八座桥梁，”阿布拉说。

sentences.en: “The only difference is that the electric utility will first need to lower the power lines and insulate them so that if animals jump from the bridge onto the wires, they won’t be electrocuted.”
sentences.zh: “唯一的区别是，电力公司首先需要降低电线并将其绝缘，这样如果动物从桥上跳到电线上，它们就不会触电。”

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>A schneider’s marmosets (Mico schneideri), a threatened species from the Amazon, crossing one of the bridges in Alta Floresta. Image courtesy of Maelle Lima de Freitas.</figcaption>

sentences.en: A schneider’s marmosets (Mico schneideri), a threatened species from the Amazon, crossing one of the bridges in Alta Floresta.
sentences.zh: 一只施奈德狨猴 (Mico schneideri) 正在穿过阿尔塔弗洛雷斯塔的一座桥梁，这是亚马逊流域的一种濒危物种。

sentences.en: Image courtesy of Maelle Lima de Freitas.
sentences.zh: 图片由 Maelle Lima de Freitas 提供。

blocks.html_en: Bridge use increases over time

sentences.en: Bridge use increases over time
sentences.zh: 桥梁的使用随着时间的推移而增加

blocks.html_en: The project underway in Alta Floresta is not Reconecta’s first. In 2021, working alongside the Waimiri-Atroari Indigenous people, the team installed 32 canopy bridges over BR-174, the highway that crosses both their territory and the Amazon between the Brazilian states of Amazonas and Roraima.

sentences.en: The project underway in Alta Floresta is not Reconecta’s first.
sentences.zh: Alta Floresta 正在进行的项目并不是 Reconecta 的第一个项目。

sentences.en: In 2021, working alongside the Waimiri-Atroari Indigenous people, the team installed 32 canopy bridges over BR-174, the highway that crosses both their territory and the Amazon between the Brazilian states of Amazonas and Roraima.
sentences.zh: 2021 年，该团队与怀米里-阿特罗阿里原住民一起，在 BR-174 上安装了 32 座天篷桥，BR-174 是一条穿越他们领土和亚马逊河流域、连接巴西亚马逊州和罗赖马州的高速公路。

blocks.html_en: The Waimiri-Atroari helped Abra identify the best locations for the artificial bridges. Over the past five years, cameras mounted on the bridge poles have documented about 1,250 primate crossings, including the golden-handed tamarin (Saguinus midas), a species that is not strictly arboreal and often descends to the ground in search of food, making it particularly vulnerable to roadkill.

sentences.en: The Waimiri-Atroari helped Abra identify the best locations for the artificial bridges.
sentences.zh: 怀米里-阿特罗里 (Waimiri-Atroari) 帮助阿布拉 (Abra) 确定了人工桥梁的最佳位置。

sentences.en: Over the past five years, cameras mounted on the bridge poles have documented about 1,250 primate crossings, including the golden-handed tamarin (Saguinus midas), a species that is not strictly arboreal and often descends to the ground in search of food, making it particularly vulnerable to roadkill.
sentences.zh: 过去五年里，安装在桥杆上的摄像机记录了大约 1,250 起灵长类动物穿越事件，其中包括金手狨猴 (Saguinus midas)，这种物种并非严格意义上的树栖动物，经常下到地面寻找食物，因此特别容易被路杀。

blocks.html_en: “The outcomes of the BR-174 project have been incredible. We were able to test different bridge designs to find out which ones the animals prefer,” Abra said.

sentences.en: “The outcomes of the BR-174 project have been incredible.
sentences.zh: “BR-174 项目的成果令人难以置信。

sentences.en: We were able to test different bridge designs to find out which ones the animals prefer,” Abra said.
sentences.zh: 我们能够测试不同的桥梁设计，以找出动物更喜欢哪种设计，”阿布拉说。

blocks.html_en: After years of working along Brazilian highways, the biologist had always sought a solution that was simple, low-cost and durable. The current bridge features a multilayer design that accommodates primates with different modes of locomotion — from species that rely on their prehensile tails to move through the canopy to brachiating species such as spider monkeys, which swing using their arms, both of which are common in the Amazon, as well as species that simply walk across the network of crossed ropes. It is worth noting that other small terrestrial mammals, including opossums, sloths and mouse opossums, have also been recorded using the bridges.

sentences.en: After years of working along Brazilian highways, the biologist had always sought a solution that was simple, low-cost and durable.
sentences.zh: 经过多年在巴西高速公路上的研究，这位生物学家一直在寻找一种简单、低成本且耐用的解决方案。

sentences.en: The current bridge features a multilayer design that accommodates primates with different modes of locomotion — from species that rely on their prehensile tails to move through the canopy to brachiating species such as spider monkeys, which swing using their arms, both of which are common in the Amazon, as well as species that simply walk across the network of crossed ropes.
sentences.zh: 目前的桥梁采用多层设计，可适应不同运动模式的灵长类动物——从依靠卷尾在树冠中移动的物种，到蜘蛛猴等用手臂摆动的臂形物种（这两种动物在亚马逊地区都很常见），以及只是在交叉绳索网络上行走的物种。

sentences.en: It is worth noting that other small terrestrial mammals, including opossums, sloths and mouse opossums, have also been recorded using the bridges.
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，其他小型陆生哺乳动物，包括负鼠、树懒和小鼠负鼠，也曾被记录使用过这些桥。

blocks.html_en: Abra said it is important to keep in mind that this is a long-term project, and results are not always immediate. BR-174 was built in the 1970s, and animals take time to adapt to a new crossing alternative. Reconecta’s monitoring has shown that use of the structures increases over time.

sentences.en: Abra said it is important to keep in mind that this is a long-term project, and results are not always immediate.
sentences.zh: 阿布拉表示，重要的是要记住，这是一个长期项目，结果并不总是立竿见影的。

sentences.en: BR-174 was built in the 1970s, and animals take time to adapt to a new crossing alternative.
sentences.zh: BR-174 建于 20 世纪 70 年代，动物需要时间来适应新的穿越替代方案。

sentences.en: Reconecta’s monitoring has shown that use of the structures increases over time.
sentences.zh: Reconecta 的监测表明，这些结构的使用随着时间的推移而增加。

blocks.html_en: “Very often, our sense of urgency in conservation doesn’t take into account the timing of the impact,” she said.

sentences.en: “Very often, our sense of urgency in conservation doesn’t take into account the timing of the impact,” she said.
sentences.zh: “很多时候，我们的保护紧迫感并没有考虑到影响的时间，”她说。

blocks.html_en: In Alta Floresta, tufted capuchins took seven months before using one of the bridges for the first time, which may indicate that each species has its own pace of adaptation.

sentences.en: In Alta Floresta, tufted capuchins took seven months before using one of the bridges for the first time, which may indicate that each species has its own pace of adaptation.
sentences.zh: 在阿尔塔弗洛雷斯塔，簇绒卷尾猴花了七个月才第一次使用其中一座桥梁，这可能表明每个物种都有自己的适应速度。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>The Alta Floresta titi monkey is the symbol of the Alta Floresta bridges program. Image courtesy of Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute and Projeto Reconecta.</figcaption>

sentences.en: The Alta Floresta titi monkey is the symbol of the Alta Floresta bridges program.
sentences.zh: 阿尔塔弗洛雷斯塔蒂蒂猴是阿尔塔弗洛雷斯塔桥梁计划的象征。

sentences.en: Image courtesy of Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute and Projeto Reconecta.
sentences.zh: 图片由史密森尼国家动物园和保护生物学研究所和 Projeto Reconecta 提供。

blocks.html_en: Species disappear, forests decline

sentences.en: Species disappear, forests decline
sentences.zh: 物种消失，森林衰退

blocks.html_en: Home to the world’s greatest diversity of primates, Brazil also has the world’s fourth-largest road network — a deadly combination for animals living along forest edges that have been split apart to make way for highways.

sentences.en: Home to the world’s greatest diversity of primates, Brazil also has the world’s fourth-largest road network — a deadly combination for animals living along forest edges that have been split apart to make way for highways.
sentences.zh: 巴西是世界上灵长类动物多样性最丰富的国家，还拥有世界第四大公路网，这对于生活在森林边缘的动物来说是一个致命的组合，这些森林边缘被分裂为高速公路。

blocks.html_en: “From our human perspective, roads mean connection and development. But for wildlife, they represent disconnection, barriers and a threat to survival,” Abra said.

sentences.en: “From our human perspective, roads mean connection and development.
sentences.zh: “从人类的角度来看，道路意味着联系和发展。

sentences.en: But for wildlife, they represent disconnection, barriers and a threat to survival,” Abra said.
sentences.zh: 但对于野生动物来说，它们代表着脱节、障碍和对生存的威胁，”阿布拉说。

blocks.html_en: Nearly 9 million mammals are killed on Brazil’s roads every year, a 2022 study found. A data compilation published in the journal Nature in 2025 also found that the country ranks among those with the highest mortality rates of terrestrial vertebrates killed in roadkills.

sentences.en: Nearly 9 million mammals are killed on Brazil’s roads every year, a 2022 study found.
sentences.zh: 2022 年的一项研究发现，巴西每年有近 900 万只哺乳动物在道路上死亡。

sentences.en: A data compilation published in the journal Nature in 2025 also found that the country ranks among those with the highest mortality rates of terrestrial vertebrates killed in roadkills.
sentences.zh: 2025年《自然》杂志发表的一份数据汇编还发现，该国是道路杀戮中陆生脊椎动物死亡率最高的国家之一。

blocks.html_en: “Habitat loss is one of the main drivers of population declines, especially among threatened species, but the impact of roadkill is still greatly underestimated,” primatologist Fabiano Rodrigues de Melo, a professor at the Federal University of Viçosa in Minas Gerais, told Mongabay.

sentences.en: “Habitat loss is one of the main drivers of population declines, especially among threatened species, but the impact of roadkill is still greatly underestimated,” primatologist Fabiano Rodrigues de Melo, a professor at the Federal University of Viçosa in Minas Gerais, told Mongabay.
sentences.zh: 米纳斯吉拉斯州维索萨联邦大学教授、灵长类动物学家法比亚诺·罗德里格斯·德梅洛 (Fabiano Rodrigues de Melo) 告诉 Mongabay：“栖息地丧失是种群数量减少的主要原因之一，尤其是受威胁物种，但道路杀戮的影响仍然被大大低估。”

blocks.html_en: Beyond roadkills, highways bring confinement to fauna.

sentences.en: Beyond roadkills, highways bring confinement to fauna.
sentences.zh: 除了道路死亡之外，高速公路还对动物群造成限制。

blocks.html_en: “When populations become isolated, they grow smaller and more vulnerable to threats,” said Amely Branquinho Martins, an environmental analyst at the ICMBio, Brazil’s federal agency for conservation units, and coordinator of the Brazilian Biodiversity Genomics (GBB) project.

sentences.en: “When populations become isolated, they grow smaller and more vulnerable to threats,” said Amely Branquinho Martins, an environmental analyst at the ICMBio, Brazil’s federal agency for conservation units, and coordinator of the Brazilian Biodiversity Genomics (GBB) project.
sentences.zh: “当种群变得孤立时，它们就会变得更小，更容易受到威胁，”巴西联邦保护机构 ICMBio 的环境分析师兼巴西生物多样性基因组学 (GBB) 项目协调员 Amely Branquinho Martins 说。

blocks.html_en: One of those threats is inbreeding, which occurs when closely related individuals reproduce. “Habitat loss and degradation can prevent an individual from one population from migrating to another — what we call gene flow. Without it, the species’ genetic diversity declines.”

sentences.en: One of those threats is inbreeding, which occurs when closely related individuals reproduce.
sentences.zh: 这些威胁之一是近亲繁殖，当密切相关的个体繁殖时就会发生这种情况。

sentences.en: “Habitat loss and degradation can prevent an individual from one population from migrating to another — what we call gene flow.
sentences.zh: “栖息地的丧失和退化可以阻止一个种群中的个体迁移到另一个种群，这就是我们所说的基因流动。

sentences.en: Without it, the species’ genetic diversity declines.”
sentences.zh: 没有它，该物种的遗传多样性就会下降。”

blocks.html_en: The consequences continue to multiply in a domino effect, Melo said. The loss of primates, especially fruit-eating species, can alter forest composition, because they play a key role in seed dispersal and even contribute to pollination.

sentences.en: The consequences continue to multiply in a domino effect, Melo said.
sentences.zh: 梅洛说，后果将继续以多米诺骨牌效应倍增。

sentences.en: The loss of primates, especially fruit-eating species, can alter forest composition, because they play a key role in seed dispersal and even contribute to pollination.
sentences.zh: 灵长类动物，特别是食果物种的消失，可能会改变森林的组成，因为它们在种子传播甚至有助于授粉方面发挥着关键作用。

blocks.html_en: “A whole network of ecological interactions is ultimately lost with the disappearance of these species,” Melo said.

sentences.en: “A whole network of ecological interactions is ultimately lost with the disappearance of these species,” Melo said.
sentences.zh: “随着这些物种的消失，整个生态相互作用网络最终消失，”梅洛说。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Biologist Fernanda Abra, founder of Reconecta. Image courtesy of Projeto Reconecta.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Biologist Fernanda Abra, founder of Reconecta.
sentences.zh: 生物学家费尔南达·阿布拉 (Fernanda Abra)，Reconecta 创始人。

sentences.en: Image courtesy of Projeto Reconecta.
sentences.zh: 图片由 Projeto Reconecta 提供。

blocks.html_en: Reconecta expands

sentences.en: Reconecta expands
sentences.zh: 重新连接扩展

blocks.html_en: One of the hopes for reducing wildlife roadkill is the passage of a bill currently under consideration in Brazil’s Congress that would establish the National Road Safety Plan for Wildlife, which calls, among other measures, for the placement of wildlife warning signs, speed-reduction devices and the construction of overpasses, aerial crossings and underpasses along highways.

sentences.en: One of the hopes for reducing wildlife roadkill is the passage of a bill currently under consideration in Brazil’s Congress that would establish the National Road Safety Plan for Wildlife, which calls, among other measures, for the placement of wildlife warning signs, speed-reduction devices and the construction of overpasses, aerial crossings and underpasses along highways.
sentences.zh: 减少野生动物道路死亡的希望之一是通过巴西国会目前正在审议的一项法案，该法案将制定国家野生动物道路安全计划，该计划除其他措施外，还要求在高速公路沿线放置野生动物警告标志、减速装置以及建造立交桥、空中交叉口和地下通道。

blocks.html_en: The bill has already been approved by the Chamber of Deputies and is now awaiting a vote in the Senate.

sentences.en: The bill has already been approved by the Chamber of Deputies and is now awaiting a vote in the Senate.
sentences.zh: 该法案已获得众议院批准，目前正等待参议院投票。

blocks.html_en: If approved, the canopy bridge model developed by Reconecta could be implemented nationwide. In 2026, Brazil’s National Department of Transport Infrastructure designated the design as the recommended standard for highways.

sentences.en: If approved, the canopy bridge model developed by Reconecta could be implemented nationwide.
sentences.zh: 如果获得批准，Reconecta开发的冠桥模型将在全国范围内推广。

sentences.en: In 2026, Brazil’s National Department of Transport Infrastructure designated the design as the recommended standard for highways.
sentences.zh: 2026年，巴西国家交通基础设施部指定该设计为高速公路推荐标准。

blocks.html_en: In addition to Reconecta expansion results, Abra’s efforts to protect primates have earned international recognition. In 2019, she received the Future For Nature Award, followed by the Whitley Award in 2024, often referred to as the Oscars of environmental conservation. In June, she was also named one of the recipients of the 2026 Wayfinder Award, joining the select group of National Geographic Explorers, who receive funding from the organization for their projects.

sentences.en: In addition to Reconecta expansion results, Abra’s efforts to protect primates have earned international recognition.
sentences.zh: 除了 Reconecta 的扩张成果外，Abra 在保护灵长类动物方面的努力也赢得了国际认可。

sentences.en: In 2019, she received the Future For Nature Award, followed by the Whitley Award in 2024, often referred to as the Oscars of environmental conservation.
sentences.zh: 2019 年，她获得了“自然未来奖”，随后于 2024 年获得了被誉为环境保护界奥斯卡的惠特利奖。

sentences.en: In June, she was also named one of the recipients of the 2026 Wayfinder Award, joining the select group of National Geographic Explorers, who receive funding from the organization for their projects.
sentences.zh: 6 月，她还被任命为 2026 年寻路者奖的获得者之一，加入了国家地理探险家的精选群体，他们的项目获得了该组织的资助。

blocks.html_en: The support comes at the right time, as Abra plans to expand Reconecta, including new chapters in Suriname and Lucas do Rio Verde, a municipality in Brazil, which will install 10 canopy bridges.

sentences.en: The support comes at the right time, as Abra plans to expand Reconecta, including new chapters in Suriname and Lucas do Rio Verde, a municipality in Brazil, which will install 10 canopy bridges.
sentences.zh: 这项支持来得正是时候，因为 Abra 计划扩大 Reconecta，包括在苏里南和巴西卢卡斯杜里约维德市开设新分会，其中将安装 10 座天篷桥。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Aerial image shows one of the bridges installed in Alta Floresta. Image courtesy of Programa Alta Floresta Não Atropela.</figcaption>

sentences.en: Aerial image shows one of the bridges installed in Alta Floresta.
sentences.zh: 航拍图像显示了阿尔塔弗洛雷斯塔安装的一座桥梁。

sentences.en: Image courtesy of Programa Alta Floresta Não Atropela.
sentences.zh: 图片由 Alta Floresta Não Atropela 计划提供。

blocks.html_en: Abra also hopes to bring her canopy bridges to other biomes, including the Pantanal and the Atlantic Forest, helping prevent the deaths of monkeys such as the buffy-headed marmoset.

sentences.en: Abra also hopes to bring her canopy bridges to other biomes, including the Pantanal and the Atlantic Forest, helping prevent the deaths of monkeys such as the buffy-headed marmoset.
sentences.zh: 阿布拉还希望将她的树冠桥带到其他生物群落，包括潘塔纳尔湿地和大西洋森林，帮助防止白头狨猴等猴子的死亡。

blocks.html_en: Expectations are especially high for bridges over BR-262, one of Brazil’s longest highways, which cuts across the Pantanal and is known as the “highway of death.” The road kills approximately 2,000 animals every year.

sentences.en: Expectations are especially high for bridges over BR-262, one of Brazil’s longest highways, which cuts across the Pantanal and is known as the “highway of death.”
sentences.zh: 人们对 BR-262 大桥的期望尤其高，BR-262 是巴西最长的高速公路之一，横跨潘塔纳尔湿地，被称为“死亡高速公路”。

sentences.en: The road kills approximately 2,000 animals every year.
sentences.zh: 这条路每年杀死大约 2,000 只动物。

blocks.html_en: “I envision a future with Reconecta Biomes. We already have Reconecta Amazon, and we’ll soon launch Reconecta Pantanal. I also see canopy bridges in the Atlantic Forest for the primates that live there, including muriquis and howler monkeys, which are both highly threatened,” she said.

sentences.en: “I envision a future with Reconecta Biomes.
sentences.zh: “我展望了 Reconecta Biomes 的未来。

sentences.en: We already have Reconecta Amazon, and we’ll soon launch Reconecta Pantanal.
sentences.zh: 我们已经有了 Reconecta Amazon，我们很快就会推出 Reconecta Pantanal。

sentences.en: I also see canopy bridges in the Atlantic Forest for the primates that live there, including muriquis and howler monkeys, which are both highly threatened,” she said.
sentences.zh: 我还在大西洋森林中看到了为生活在那里的灵长类动物建造的树冠桥，包括鼠猴和吼猴，它们都受到高度威胁，”她说。

blocks.html_en: She stressed that for this vision to succeed, the continued engagement of government agencies, private companies and local communities will be essential, just as it was in Alta Floresta and the Waimiri-Atroari Indigenous Territory. “When everyone is involved, everyone values the initiative and works harder to make it succeed.”

sentences.en: She stressed that for this vision to succeed, the continued engagement of government agencies, private companies and local communities will be essential, just as it was in Alta Floresta and the Waimiri-Atroari Indigenous Territory.
sentences.zh: 她强调，要实现这一愿景，政府机构、私营公司和当地社区的持续参与至关重要，就像上弗洛雷斯塔和怀米里-阿特罗阿里原住民领地的情况一样。

sentences.en: “When everyone is involved, everyone values the initiative and works harder to make it succeed.”
sentences.zh: “当每个人都参与进来时，每个人都会重视这一举措，并更加努力地使其成功。”

blocks.html_en: Banner image: A black-faced black spider monkey (Ateles chamek) crossing one of the multilayered bridges. Image courtesy of Sergio Leal.

content_html_zh:
• Reconecta 项目在巴西的一个城市安装了八座索桥，一项为期 15 个月的研究使用相机陷阱记录了数千种树栖野生动物如何适应新的空中穿越。
  • 安全的道路通道可以促进种子传播和基因多样性，扭转因高速公路分裂栖息地而产生的生物多样性丧失的“多米诺骨牌效应”。
  • Reconecta 的创建者、生物学家费尔南达·阿布拉 (Fernanda Abra)、国家地理探险家和惠特利奖获得者，目前正在将这些低成本保护工具扩展到其他巴西生物群落和邻国苏里南。
  • 巴西联邦交通部门已正式将索桥设计指定为高速公路的推荐国家标准，以遏制每年数以百万计的野生动物在道路上死亡的情况。

早上 7 点左右，浓雾笼罩大地，生物学家加布里埃尔·法尔奎托 (Gabriel Falquetto) 在巴西圣埃斯皮里图州的 ES-164 高速公路沿线发现了几只被路杀的动物。 他正在前往位于大西洋森林的凯特斯私人自然遗产保护区 (RPPN) 的途中，在那里他监测该地区的野生动物和路杀动物。

突然，他发现人行道上有一具灵长类动物的尸体。 这是一只黄头狨猴（Callithrix flaviceps），被国际自然保护联盟（IUCN）红色名录列为极度濒危物种。 据估计，野生动物中仍有 2,500 只，使其成为世界上受威胁最严重的灵长类动物之一。

“这是极其痛苦和令人沮丧的，”法尔奎托告诉 Mongabay。 “我们每天都会穿越这个地区，监测野生动物，并庆祝每次在森林中遇到成群的棕头狨猴。”

“在这种情况下找到一个人会让人感到悲伤和无助。 除了这只动物的损失之外，这一事件还提醒我们我们的活动对生物多样性造成的影响。”

大约 2,900 公里（1,800 英里）外，在通往马托格罗索州最北部亚马逊小镇阿尔塔弗洛雷斯塔 (Alta Floresta) 的道路上，自 2024 年以来就没有记录到像黄头狨猴被路杀的案例。 那一年，安装了八座树冠桥，连接零散的森林斑块。 摄像机捕捉到的镜头显示，在 15 个多月的时间里，野生动物空中穿越通道实现了 15,000 次树栖动物的安全穿越，没有造成一例道路死亡。

这些桥梁是 Projeto Reconecta 和市政府以及当地组织和企业主参与的计划的一部分，他们渴望保护城镇独特的生物多样性并促进生态旅游，但对越来越多的野生动物与车辆碰撞事件感到震惊。

美国史密森尼国家动物园和保护生物学研究所副研究员、Reconecta 创始人、生物学家费尔南达·阿布拉 (Fernanda Abra) 回忆道，就在上弗洛雷斯塔安装第一座桥梁的前几个小时，她发现两只施耐德狨猴 (Mico schneideri) 死亡。 该物种是巴西亚马逊地区的特有物种，面临灭绝的威胁。

“天篷桥有两个目的，”阿布拉说。 “首先是改善野生动物的连通性，允许动物在这些支离破碎的森林地区从一侧移动到另一侧。 因此，我们也减少了道路交通事故。”

所有桥梁都有两个相机陷阱，一个面向十字路口以记录哪些动物正在使用它，另一个面向森林以观察哪些动物到达那里但决定不使用它。

在该计划的头几个月里，使用桥梁记录到的灵长类动物包括黑面黑蜘蛛猴 (Ateles chamek)、红吼猴 (Alouatta puruensis)、北夜猴 (Aotus infulatus)、簇绒卷尾猴 (Sapajus apella)、施耐德狨猴和阿尔塔花猴 (Plecturocebus grovesi)。

Alta Floresta 蒂蒂猴在 2019 年才被描述为极度濒危物种，不久后就已成为该倡议的象征，由于其积极成果，该倡议目前正在进入一个新阶段。

“八月份，我们将在该市市区再安装八座桥梁，”阿布拉说。 “唯一的区别是，电力公司首先需要降低电线并将其绝缘，这样如果动物从桥上跳到电线上，它们就不会触电。”

桥梁的使用随着时间的推移而增加

Alta Floresta 正在进行的项目并不是 Reconecta 的第一个项目。 2021 年，该团队与怀米里-阿特罗阿里原住民一起，在 BR-174 上安装了 32 座天篷桥，BR-174 是一条穿越他们领土和亚马逊河流域、连接巴西亚马逊州和罗赖马州的高速公路。

怀米里-阿特罗里 (Waimiri-Atroari) 帮助阿布拉 (Abra) 确定了人工桥梁的最佳位置。 过去五年里，安装在桥杆上的摄像机记录了大约 1,250 起灵长类动物穿越事件，其中包括金手狨猴 (Saguinus midas)，这种物种并非严格意义上的树栖动物，经常下到地面寻找食物，因此特别容易被路杀。

“BR-174 项目的成果令人难以置信。 我们能够测试不同的桥梁设计，以找出动物更喜欢哪种设计，”阿布拉说。

经过多年在巴西高速公路上的研究，这位生物学家一直在寻找一种简单、低成本且耐用的解决方案。 目前的桥梁采用多层设计，可适应不同运动模式的灵长类动物——从依靠卷尾在树冠中移动的物种，到蜘蛛猴等用手臂摆动的臂形物种（这两种动物在亚马逊地区都很常见），以及只是在交叉绳索网络上行走的物种。 值得注意的是，其他小型陆生哺乳动物，包括负鼠、树懒和小鼠负鼠，也曾被记录使用过这些桥。

阿布拉表示，重要的是要记住，这是一个长期项目，结果并不总是立竿见影的。 BR-174 建于 20 世纪 70 年代，动物需要时间来适应新的穿越替代方案。 Reconecta 的监测表明，这些结构的使用随着时间的推移而增加。

“很多时候，我们的保护紧迫感并没有考虑到影响的时间，”她说。

在阿尔塔弗洛雷斯塔，簇绒卷尾猴花了七个月才第一次使用其中一座桥梁，这可能表明每个物种都有自己的适应速度。

物种消失，森林衰退

巴西是世界上灵长类动物多样性最丰富的国家，还拥有世界第四大公路网，这对于生活在森林边缘的动物来说是一个致命的组合，这些森林边缘被分裂为高速公路。

“从人类的角度来看，道路意味着联系和发展。 但对于野生动物来说，它们代表着脱节、障碍和对生存的威胁，”阿布拉说。

2022 年的一项研究发现，巴西每年有近 900 万只哺乳动物在道路上死亡。 2025年《自然》杂志发表的一份数据汇编还发现，该国是道路杀戮中陆生脊椎动物死亡率最高的国家之一。

米纳斯吉拉斯州维索萨联邦大学教授、灵长类动物学家法比亚诺·罗德里格斯·德梅洛 (Fabiano Rodrigues de Melo) 告诉 Mongabay：“栖息地丧失是种群数量减少的主要原因之一，尤其是受威胁物种，但道路杀戮的影响仍然被大大低估。”

除了道路死亡之外，高速公路还对动物群造成限制。

“当种群变得孤立时，它们就会变得更小，更容易受到威胁，”巴西联邦保护机构 ICMBio 的环境分析师兼巴西生物多样性基因组学 (GBB) 项目协调员 Amely Branquinho Martins 说。

这些威胁之一是近亲繁殖，当密切相关的个体繁殖时就会发生这种情况。 “栖息地的丧失和退化可以阻止一个种群中的个体迁移到另一个种群，这就是我们所说的基因流动。 没有它，该物种的遗传多样性就会下降。”

梅洛说，后果将继续以多米诺骨牌效应倍增。 灵长类动物，特别是食果物种的消失，可能会改变森林的组成，因为它们在种子传播甚至有助于授粉方面发挥着关键作用。

“随着这些物种的消失，整个生态相互作用网络最终消失，”梅洛说。

重新连接扩展

减少野生动物道路死亡的希望之一是通过巴西国会目前正在审议的一项法案，该法案将制定国家野生动物道路安全计划，该计划除其他措施外，还要求在高速公路沿线放置野生动物警告标志、减速装置以及建造立交桥、空中交叉口和地下通道。

该法案已获得众议院批准，目前正等待参议院投票。

如果获得批准，Reconecta开发的冠桥模型将在全国范围内推广。 2026年，巴西国家交通基础设施部指定该设计为高速公路推荐标准。

除了 Reconecta 的扩张成果外，Abra 在保护灵长类动物方面的努力也赢得了国际认可。 2019 年，她获得了“自然未来奖”，随后于 2024 年获得了被誉为环境保护界奥斯卡的惠特利奖。 6 月，她还被任命为 2026 年寻路者奖的获得者之一，加入了国家地理探险家的精选群体，他们的项目获得了该组织的资助。

这项支持来得正是时候，因为 Abra 计划扩大 Reconecta，包括在苏里南和巴西卢卡斯杜里约维德市开设新分会，其中将安装 10 座天篷桥。

阿布拉还希望将她的树冠桥带到其他生物群落，包括潘塔纳尔湿地和大西洋森林，帮助防止白头狨猴等猴子的死亡。

人们对 BR-262 大桥的期望尤其高，BR-262 是巴西最长的高速公路之一，横跨潘塔纳尔湿地，被称为“死亡高速公路”。 这条路每年杀死大约 2,000 只动物。

“我展望了 Reconecta Biomes 的未来。 我们已经有了 Reconecta Amazon，我们很快就会推出 Reconecta Pantanal。 我还在大西洋森林中看到了为生活在那里的灵长类动物建造的树冠桥，包括鼠猴和吼猴，它们都受到高度威胁，”她说。

她强调，要实现这一愿景，政府机构、私营公司和当地社区的持续参与至关重要，就像上弗洛雷斯塔和怀米里-阿特罗阿里原住民领地的情况一样。 “当每个人都参与进来时，每个人都会重视这一举措，并更加努力地使其成功。”

横幅图片：一只黑脸黑蜘蛛猴 (Ateles chamek) 穿过其中一座多层桥。 图片由塞尔吉奥·莱尔提供。

天篷桥到底有多有效？
